p1_vlm_decision.reasoning: The bed model is correctly oriented when the mattress surface is horizontal and facing upward, and the headboard is vertical and at the head end. In G5, the model is lying on its side with the mattress surface horizontal but the headboard oriented sideways, making it incorrect. In G6, the mattress is horizontal and facing upward, the headboard is vertical and at the head end, and the base rests naturally on the ground plane. This matches the functional upright position of a bed. The other groups (G1-G4) show the model tilted or rotated such that the mattress is not the top surface or the headboard is not upright. Therefore, G6 is the only group showing the bed in its natural upright standing position.

p2_vlm_decision.reasoning: The front face of a bed is the headboard side, which is typically taller and more decorative. In Angle 1 (0°), the headboard is clearly visible and facing the camera directly. In Angle 2 (90°) and Angle 4 (270°), the side rails are visible, indicating a side view. In Angle 3 (180°), the footboard is facing the camera, which is the back of the bed. Therefore, Angle 1 shows the front face directly facing the camera.
